title: Valid row-value syntax is rejected
description:
drh added on 2018-01-23 17:59:29:
The SELECT statement below if valid syntax and ought to work, but SQLite rejects
it and refuses to evaluate it:
CREATE TABLE x(a, b, PRIMARY KEY (a,b));
CREATE TABLE y(a);
CREATE TABLE z(a, b);
SELECT * FROM x JOIN y ON y.a = x.a  WHERE (x.a, x.b) IN (SELECT a, b FROM z);
This problem has been in the code since support for row-values was added
with version 3.15.0 (2016-10-14).  SQLite does not generate an incorrect
answer because of this bug.  Rather, SQLite merely refuses to process what
ought to be valid SQL syntax.
Note that this problem was fixed prior to the ticket being created.  The
ticket was added after-the-fact for documentation purposes.
drh added on 2018-01-23 18:00:58:
Forgot to say:  The problem was reported on the sqlite-users mailing list
by Mark Brand.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE a(id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, age INT CHECK(age >= 0));
CREATE TABLE b(id INT PRIMARY KEY, a_id INT, FOREIGN KEY(a_id) REFERENCES a(id));
CREATE TABLE c(id INT PRIMARY KEY, value FLOAT DEFAULT 0.0);
INSERT INTO a(name, age) VALUES ('Alice', 30), ('Bob', NULL), ('Charlie', -1);
SELECT * FROM a JOIN b ON a.id = b.a_id WHERE (a.id, a.age) IN (SELECT id, age FROM a WHERE age IS NOT NULL);
key_bug_features: SQLite rejects valid row-value syntax in SELECT statement
